meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve no version control (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Solve No Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master API integration for version control. A pro-level tutorial for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracking API states. By abstracting your API layer into a version-controlled middleware using Antigravity and Claude Code, you decouple logic from endpoints. This creates a single source of truth, allowing you to rollback, audit, and deploy robust integrations without fearing breaking changes in your production codebase.
content.intro:
You are staring at a production codebase that feels like a house of cards. A single unauthorized API tweak from a third-party provider just nuked your client’s dashboard, and because you lack robust version control for your integration layer, you are scrambling to find the diffs. The clock is ticking, the client is pinging you on Slack, and your reputation is on the line.

This isn't just a coding problem; it's a structural failure in how you manage external dependencies. Today, we turn that chaos into a repeatable, version-controlled architecture. We will use Antigravity to define your API schemas and Claude Code to automate the migration, ensuring your integrations are as stable as your core application logic.

steps.title: Define Schema with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a rigid contract for your API responses. This prevents 'schema drift' where API changes break your app silently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a strictly typed schema for the target API using the latest OpenAPI specification. Include error handling definitions and version headers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a schema file based on the target API documentation provided in the context folder.'

steps.title: Initialize Versioned Middleware
steps.description: Create a dedicated directory for API versions. This allows you to run multiple integration versions concurrently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a directory structure for versioned API wrappers (v1, v2) including a factory pattern to switch between versions dynamically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a versioned directory structure in /src/api and implement a factory pattern to switch versions.'

steps.title: Automate Endpoint Mapping
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your internal functions to the API endpoints defined in your schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the internal application methods to the schema endpoints. Ensure every request includes a version tag in the header.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Map all methods in /src/services to the API endpoints defined in the schema file.'

steps.title: Implement Automated Regression Tests
steps.description: Ensure your integrations haven't broken by running tests against mock API responses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate integration tests that use the schema definitions to validate incoming API payloads against expected types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Generate and run integration tests for all API endpoints using the schema as the source of truth.'

steps.title: Deploy with Version Locking
steps.description: Finalize your deployment pipeline to lock API versions, preventing accidental upgrades to production integrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a deployment script that validates the API version tag against the production configuration before pushing to the staging environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a CI/CD check script that fails if the API versioning in the code doesn't match the production configuration.'

pro_tips: Always wrap third-party API calls in an 'Adapter' pattern; never call the SDK directly from your UI components.
Use a 'Circuit Breaker' pattern for external APIs to prevent cascading failures when a provider goes down.
Store API response snapshots in your repository as JSON fixtures to create a 'time-machine' for debugging integration issues.
Implement request logging with correlation IDs to track a single user action through your entire API integration stack.
Automate the generation of your API documentation from your Antigravity schema files to keep client deliverables updated effortlessly.
content.next_steps: Once your versioned middleware is running, set up a GitHub Action to run your integration test suite on every pull request to ensure no breaking changes slip into production.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on manual refactors and start building scalable, professional-grade integrations that keep your clients happy and your code clean. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
